title: Rival-aware undercut/overcut opportunity features
summary: F1 strategy references emphasize that undercut/overcut only matter when a driver is within striking distance, tyre warm-up is favorable, and traffic/clean air cooperate.
rationale: F1 strategy references emphasize that undercut/overcut only matter when a driver is within striking distance, tyre warm-up is favorable, and traffic/clean air cooperate. The current top features model tyre state well, but it does not explicitly encode same-lap local rivalry or clean-air opportunity.
implementation_hint: Within each `(Year, Race, LapNumber)` snapshot, derive local field features: gap in `LapTime (s)` or `LapTime_Delta` to nearest cars ahead/behind in position order, percentile ranks of degradation and lap delta within the lap, local traffic density around a driver’s position, and binaries like `Within_Undercut_Window` when pace gap proxies are small. Feed these to a classifier or a small query model grouped by lap snapshot or nearby-car subsets only.
expected_effect: Could recover missed pit calls driven by tactical battles rather than absolute tyre age, especially on tracks where undercut sensitivity is high.
risk: No explicit interval timing gaps are provided, so proxies may be noisy and group rankers can still underperform if queries are too broad.
